title: ctx_ptr safety comment omits the pinning invariant
description: client.rs ctx_ptr() uses get_unchecked_mut() to obtain a mutable pointer through a Pin<Box<ClientInner>>. The safety comment says 'We only expose a pointer, we never move through it' but does not explain why get_unchecked_mut is sound here: the Pin guarantee is that the ClientInner is heap-allocated and will never be moved; the pointer obtained is valid for the lifetime of the pin, not just the call. A reader who knows Pin semantics will understand eventually, but the comment invites confusion about why this specific unsafe fn is safe rather than, say, just using &mut self.inner.client_ctx via DerefMut. What would make it clear: add a comment explaining that Pin<Box<T>> guarantees the Box heap allocation is stable, so the raw pointer into client_ctx remains valid as long as the Pin exists — and that get_unchecked_mut is needed because Pin<Box<T>> does not implement DerefMut to its interior.
